failure_modes.text: Automatic behavior can keep solving yesterday's problem when the environment has changed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in environment-change triggers that interrupt automatic behavior when the conditions the habit was built for have shifted.

failure_modes.text: Entrenched routines can filter evidence and protect the habit instead of the intended outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: entrenched behavioral patterns selectively filter evidence
failure_modes.mitigation: Test habits against current evidence: entrenched routines can filter information to protect the habit rather than the intended outcome.

failure_modes.text: Strategy stays inert when the required behaviors are never converted into repeatable routines.
failure_modes.mitigation: Convert strategic intent into repeatable routines: strategy stays inert until the required behaviors become habitual.

premortem_questions.text: What cue, feeling, or belief is firing right before the unwanted behavior starts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are you feeling in the moment right before you begin the behavior?

premortem_questions.text: Does this routine still serve the goal, or is it only protecting old comfort?
Which small repeated behavior would most improve reliability under stress?

heuristics.text: Use cue, craving, response, and reward to redesign the loop rather than relying on vague intention.
Scaffold the response into small repeatable units so the behavior becomes easier to perform.
Design for consistency so useful behavior survives fatigue and overload.
